summary: Promoted the runtime-general Homepage and Uptime Kuma recovery into canonical mainline truth as repository version `0.178.2` and platform version `0.130.98` after replaying `runtime-general-pool` from the latest realistic `origin/main` baseline at `0.178.1` / `0.130.97`, restoring migrated Uptime Kuma state, repairing the Compose-plugin and preflight contract drift, pinning Docker guest public-edge aliases, binding Homepage on loopback for same-guest Traefik, and re-verifying the authenticated `home.lv3.org` plus uptime/status routes while preserving the unrelated `controller_dependency_gap x3` release-manager blocker transparently.

verification.check: Protected-surface closeout on the latest exact mainline
verification.observed: The exact-main blocker snapshot in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-ws-0332-mainline-release-status-r1.json` preserved the unrelated repo-wide waiver debt at `controller_dependency_gap x3`, `...release-dry-run-r1.txt` confirmed the `0.178.1 -> 0.178.2` cut, `...release-r1.txt` preserved the expected top-level release-manager refusal, and `...release-lower-level-r1-0.178.2.txt` recorded the lower-level canonical-truth and release-note write that updated the protected `0.178.2` / `0.130.98` surfaces without claiming the unrelated blockers were fixed.

verification.check: Exact-main runtime-general replay and governed backup refresh
verification.observed: `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true make live-apply-service service=runtime-general-pool env=production EXTRA_ARGS='-e bypass_promotion=true'` completed successfully with recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=4 changed=0 failed=0`, `monitoring-lv3 : ok=38 changed=0 failed=0`, `nginx-lv3 : ok=71 changed=5 failed=0`, `proxmox_florin : ok=41 changed=10 failed=0`, and `runtime-general-lv3 : ok=322 changed=7 failed=0`, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-ws-0332-mainline-live-apply-r1.txt`; the same replay refreshed `receipts/restic-backups/20260403T162652Z.json` and `receipts/restic-snapshots-latest.json`.

verification.check: Public Homepage, Uptime Kuma, and status routes after the replay
verification.observed: External verification preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-ws-0332-public-routes-r1.txt` confirmed `https://home.lv3.org` returns the expected oauth2-proxy `302`, `https://uptime.lv3.org` returns `302` to `/dashboard`, `https://uptime.lv3.org/dashboard` returns `200`, and `https://status.lv3.org` returns `200`.

The exact-main pytest slice across the runtime-general playbook and the Homepage, Uptime Kuma, and Docker runtime roles passed with `25 passed in 27.94s`, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-03-ws-0332-targeted-tests-r1.txt`.

verification.check: Exact-main repository validation and generated-surface bundle
verification.observed: After promoting ws-0332 into the protected `0.178.2` / `0.130.98` release tree and making the generated README title generic enough for the public-entrypoint policy, the exact-main candidate passed `scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate`, `scripts/live_apply_receipts.py --validate`, `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards`, `./scripts/validate_repo.sh generated-docs`, `./scripts/validate_repo.sh generated-portals`, `./scripts/validate_repo.sh workstream-surfaces`, and `git diff --check`, preserved in the `...validate-data-models-r1.txt`, `...live-apply-receipts-validate-r2.txt`, `...agent-standards-r2.txt`, `...generated-docs-r2.txt`, `...generated-portals-r1.txt`, `...workstream-surfaces-r2.txt`, and `...git-diff-check-r3.txt` evidence files.

notes: This receipt is the canonical proof for the ADR 0152 recovery correction loop on repository version `0.178.2` and platform version `0.130.98`.
The one-time Uptime Kuma legacy-data restore is intentionally guarded by the runtime-general marker so later exact-main replays stay idempotent after the migrated state is restored.

The top-level `release_manager` blocker remains unrelated historical waiver debt for `controller_dependency_gap`; this workstream preserved that blocker honestly and used the same lower-level release-helper precedent already established on `main` for protected-surface closeout.

The final exact-main release tree also keeps the ws-0332 title generic in README-facing generated surfaces so `agent-standards` stays green without removing the deployment-specific route evidence from the workstream and receipt records where it belongs.

correction_loop.surface: exact-main Homepage, Uptime Kuma, and protected release closeout
correction_loop.repair_action: Rebound Homepage on loopback for same-guest Traefik, converted Docker guest public-edge aliases into a managed block, repaired the Uptime Kuma role's Compose-plugin and preflight contract, restored the legacy Uptime Kuma data directory during the first successful runtime-general replay, re-verified the public routes, and then wrote the protected release and canonical-truth surfaces through the lower-level helpers after the top-level release manager correctly refused the unrelated waiver blockers.
